mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and investment demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in a multivariate regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, or the predictive coefficient turns negative when conditioned on recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth rates consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared drops below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields negative or flat 60-day forward returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth dropping below 0.5 percent annually signals severe economic stagnation, which compresses corporate cash flow forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is under 0.5%, or the threshold model fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index levels reflect stronger aggregate supply and economic activity, which correlates with elevated corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO levels show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, or the correlation coefficient remains below 0.05 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.
